TextENGNew: "...You still have as large an appetite as ever, huh, Minai?@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1011. That's way more than the cost of treating a lady to dinner."
"Hehe... Come on, I told you to pick a cheap restaurant, didn't I?"
Smiling and laughing, Tomoe playfully winked at him. After seeing that, Tsukada simply shrugged and let out a sigh.

"That restaurant is really popular and hard to get a reservation at, you know?@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1013. And yet, you looked at the menu and asked "can you make it an extra-large portion?" Is that normal?@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1014. And even though it was a full course meal, you even asked for additional items... Sheesh."
"I thought it would make it easier to suggest we split the bill.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2335. It's not too late for me to pay you back for my portion, if you'd like?"
"Hah, don't be ridiculous!@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1016. It'd be far too embarrassing to do that after inviting you out here, so I won't allow it."
Tsukada crossed his arms as he said that and snorted while puffing out his chest.
...Then, he suddenly remembered something and thought it was amusing, so he covered his mouth with his hand to hide his laughter.
"...What is it?"

"Oh, it's just that I used to often treat you like this after club activities or after finishing up student council work.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1018. But at the time, we always went for takoyaki, ikayaki, okonomiyaki, or yakisoba.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1019. ...Everything battered and fried."
"Back then, I could completely fill my stomach for just 100 yen.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2338. Seems the popular item for kids today is to go out for hamburgers."

"Times are always changing.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1021. Back then, I couldn't even imagine buying dinner at a convenience store open 24 hours a day.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1022. We had to deal with all sorts of hardships without that."
"Yeah..."
...Then, she remembered. It was back in high school, when they traveled to some far off town for the inter-high athletic meet.
Tomoe was feeling hungry in the middle of the night, and snuck off to the dormitory kitchen to swipe some food... only to find Tsukada there for the exact same reason, munching on a cup of noodles.
"In the end, there wasn't any rice or bread... and so I was about to head back disappointed, but then you gave me that ramen.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2341. ...Although it was half-eaten by that point."

"Don't say it like that.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1024. That was my hard-earned reward for slipping past the surveillance network of teachers outside our dorms and sneaking into the closed dry goods shop, y'know?"
"And yet I gave you half of it... No, really, I only took two bites of it.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1026. I don't think it would have killed you to bow down in gratitude."
"Ahahaha..."
Still, could that have been considered an indirect kiss? ...Tomoe remembered how she realized that after eating it, and suddenly started feeling heated.
And on the way home from the tournament, the athletic advisor gave everyone ramen as a way of celebrating, but Tsukada came over to her and said "you owe me" then robbed her of three servings.
The other track team members who saw that began spreading rumors that "Minai and Tsukada are dating," which made things difficult for a while...

"Your fan club held a pretty strong grudge against me.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2344. No matter how many times I tried to deny it, they didn't believe me at all.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2345. Even though if they'd been paying attention, they probably would've realized it wasn't true."
"Well... I didn't really try to deny it."
"In fact, even from the beginning I thought it might be nice to have that sort of relationship.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1029. ...And I certainly didn't think you were a bad choice, Minai."
Feeling her cheeks start to heat up, Tomoe quickly turned to look away.
...Tomoe, too, was half-lying when she said she denied it at the time. She really was embarrassed and didn't fully understand Tsukada's feelings, so she kept her words rather vague.
(What if, back then...)
If Tsukada had confessed how he felt.
Or if she had a little more courage and a positive attitude.
What would they be like now...? Tomoe thought about it for a moment, but immediately chased the thought away.
That was just the imagination of a dreaming girl. Nowadays, they had far less in common.
But... encountering her first love after such a long time did make her feel nostalgic for her time in school.
...With that in mind, Tomoe couldn't help dreaming up those childish delusions.
"...Back in the old days, we often used to go home together like this, didn't we?"
"Yeah.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1031. ...But no matter how many times I asked you out, you never went along with it.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1032. I figured there must've been someone else you liked."

"Back then, I was completely obsessed with the track club.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2352. I enjoyed looking to improve my personal best in every competition, and I never really thought about doing other things."
That also just an excuse. In reality, she wasn't sure how to act if she met Tsukada anywhere outside of usual club activities.
She lacked the courage to step forward and change things. Even if the end result wasn't what she wanted, it wouldn't have been that big a deal... but she was very timid at the time.
Realizing how pathetic it sounded, Tomoe bitterly smiled to herself.
(But... at any rate, I chose to focus on the track team back then.@k@-@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2354. And that's fine...)
And because of those past experiences she now understood the value of moving forward, so she was better able to provide counseling for people like Natsumi.
To only regret it would be sad, but reflecting on past failures can avoid problems in the present... and build a solid foundation for your future.
Maybe Tomoe was just taking a defiant attitude... but she wanted to believe that was the truth.

"That makes sense.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1034. You really did devote your life to the high jump back then.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1035. ...Did you ever hear?@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1036. A lot of college track and field coaches came out to the last tournament to see us."
"Yeah, I heard about that from a friend.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2356. And the guidance counselor said I received a lot of recommendations."
"But, I just enjoyed improving my personal records... so I never really thought about using it as a stepping stone for college."

In fact, after her parents' death and Madoka's sudden illness changed the course of her life... Tomoe refused to accept any of the recommendation-based scholarships she received so casually that she even worried the guidance counselor, Tsukada, and the others.
Even though track was the one thing she was passionate about, she didn't feel any regrets.
(...I didn't mean to devote my life to track, so why was I so desperate?)
Thinking about it now, it was strange.
Looking back on her time as a student, she paid little thought to becoming an adult. Her dreams of the future... like becoming a stewardess were just something she longed for.
(So, it wasn't so much that I was enjoying my time as a student... I just took it easy?)
She wasn't thinking about the future, or anyone other than herself... she just devoted all her time and her thoughts toward enhancing the present.
She had nothing to be responsible for, so she became absorbed in the activity she loved and paid the minimum amount of focus to everything else.
...It was only after she became a member of society that she realized that was a luxury she could only afford in her student days. That was when she learned what it meant to become an adult.
With that in mind, she wondered if she should've made more of an effort toward her future outside of club activities, and felt like talking about her past self.

"In that regard, you were far more diligent, Tsukada-senpai.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2361. Becoming a politician is a huge goal.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2362. You got into a good university and built up your personal connections in between your studies, all in the pursuit of that goal."
"Hahaha, did I ever say that?"
"You did. I remember.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2364. You said your dream was to become a cabinet minister and to use your great power to bring happiness to the people."
"My dream... huh?@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1039. There was a time I believed in that."
Tsukada continued walking and gazed up at the starry sky as he muttered that.
...She couldn't help but think that the side of his face looked a little lonely in the darkness of the night.
"So, are things not going well?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2366. With your secretary job, I mean..."

"Oh, it's fine.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1041. Chiba-sensei is feeling especially enthusiastic about the upcoming election, and the election office is all ready to go.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1042. There are many people who've supported him for many years, and he has a solid, proven track record.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1043. Next time he might go for cabinet minister... or maybe even deputy prime minister."
"Ahaha, that's amazing.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2368. ...So, does that mean it'll be your turn in the next election, Tsukada-sensei?"

"Me?@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1045. ...Haha, it's way too soon for that.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1046. I'd like to become a congressman, but the upper house is completely full and we're still waiting on some available seats in the lower house."
"And what's more, the senior members are staying put, so the number of open seats is decreasing year after year.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1048. ...Who knows when my turn might come around."
"Senpai..."
"It seems that once you're elected a member of congress, it's hard to quit.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1050. It's not like the student council, where members are replaced every year."
Even though Tomoe half meant that as a joke, Tsukada's unexpected display of emotion made Tomoe stop playing around.
The Osamu Tsukada she knew was confident and flirtatious... not someone to be frustrated by hardship. Was he hitting such a huge wall here to make him adopt such a negative view?
"...But weren't you the one who said you'd slice down those old dogs?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2371. Like the Iron Chancellor you admired."
"Come on that's Bismarck's title.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1052. From Prussia.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1053. I prefer the..."
"Hee hee... I remember.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2373. You wanted to be like that British politician who overhauled the monarchy and formed the basis of parliamentary politics, right?"
Tomoe intentionally got it wrong to soften the mood, and smiled.
Oliver Cromwell, a politician who led the Puritans to fight against the tyranny of their king, and significantly contributed to strengthening the power of parliament.

At the time, it was insane and reckless to consider challenging an opponent who was regarded as an incarnation of God... People like Tomoe looking back on it hundreds of years later couldn't possibly understand.
But, he was able to achieve it. With the exceptional power and cooperation of his many supporters.
It was one of the most groundbreaking events in terms of civil revolutions. In fact, the later American Revolutionary War and the French Revolution wouldn't have happened without it.

And when Tsukada was a student, that was the historical figure he looked up to. After all, any time the topic of politics came up he began passionately discussing it. Although, Tomoe was bewildered and only half-listened...
"But even such a strong man eventually became a dictator and dissolved the parliament.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2375. And he established a hereditary system that would allow his son to rule."
"...When you think about it like that, it makes you wonder what the right thing to do politically really is."
"...Indeed.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1055. Minai, you could argue that what he did wasn't a revolution at all... but rather, a coup."
"I'm not denying all his achievements.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2378. ...But if there's any possibility of a revolution without bloodshed, I think that would be ideal."
"Certainly.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1057. ...But it was an unavoidable choice in an era before democratic politics had taken root."

"After all, there aren't many people who can think and move entirely of their own gumption.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1059. They'd prefer to leave responsibility to someone else and follow along, if possible."
"It's very hard to convince someone who holds different opinions than your own... As I approach that position, I feel that pain again and again."
"...I bet."
Tomoe let out a bitter sigh as she agreed with him.
Even as a member of the police force, she always felt frustrated by obstructions. Even when she's convinced she's in the right, it still takes a lot of effort to earn the cooperation of superiors.
And even when they do move... their support and expectations become shackles, and can even make you lose the opportunity to get what you want.
That tradeoff was Tomoe's personal dilemma, and it would eternally serve as an obstacle in future activities.

Moreover, there's a significant amount of delay before you can even become a politician. And even if you're lucky enough to be elected to the position, there are rival factions, disputes with opposition parties, and dealings with bureaucrats awaiting you there...
For a while, the two remained silent while walking toward the parking lot where Tsukada's car was parked.
Somehow, they had entered a topic neither one wanted to broach, so they both remained silent.
But... while they were talking, Tomoe actually noticed something strange.
(We haven't said a word about police work this whole time...)

She thought back to the start of the meal. The conversation topics ranged from reminiscing about the track club and the student council, to the contents of Tsukada's work as a secretary for a congressman... but never anything about Tomoe's profession.
She didn't want to boast about her work as a police officer, but she intended to answer any questions when asked, within the scope she was allowed.
And yet, Tsukada didn't say a single thing about it, even as a joke. ...Which further heightened her suspicions about the reason he invited her out to eat.
(So... what should I do?)
When they left the restaurant, Tsukada offered to give her a drive... but when she set aside her past memories and thought it over, she decided she'd rather refrain.
She knew from the information Arakawa provided that there were some big names in the pharmaceutical world involved in distributing the medicine Natsumi and Reina were taking.

Considering that, a former Welfare Minister like Chiba was one of the targets of suspicion in the investigation. Moreover, his secretary Tsukada contacted her right at the same time. The timing was just too perfect.
(If I got in his car, where might he take me...?)
She certainly did want to trust Tsukada. ...But looking back, that naivete was the reason she was led astray by Hanada's betrayal.
She had to be especially careful here... and thinking that, she thought it would be best to decline, but that moment...
"Hey, Minai."
"Ah, yes...?"
"...How much have you uncovered about Lowell Inc?"
Tomoe gasped and quickly turned toward Tsukada, then stopped in place.
He walked another three steps to stay outside of her line of sight... then stopped, with his back still turned toward her.
"Tsukada-senpai...?"

"...I hate to use the word fate.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1065. I believe the notion that opportunities are created by someone else's will, rather than through one's own talent and effort, is just an excuse made by the lazy and weak of will."
"But, now... I'm starting to want to change my mind just a little.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1067. Now that an old acquaintance is standing in my way like this..."
And then.
Tsukada slowly turned around... gazing at Tomoe with a chilling look on his face that made his earlier demeanor seem like a fabrication.
There was no smile, no bloodlust... nothing.
A completely transparent, colorless mood was being conveyed, so Tomoe reflexively set herself on guard.

"Minai.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1069. I was surprised to learn you were a detective... but I couldn't believe you'd gotten involved in the Lowell case.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1070. God can be so wicked, finding amusement in making us act out this farce..."
"...So it's true then?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2390. You... were already involved in this when we met half a year ago?"

"That's right.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1072. ...For probably five years now.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1073. I've been involved in all sorts of things since I finished my master's degree and started working at Chiba-sensei's election office.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1074. And among those things, one was... that."
"Tsukada-san...!"
Tomoe tightly clenched her fists, while biting her lip so hard she was practically eating it.
...She didn't want to believe it.
No matter how suspicious he was, even if she heard the facts directly from the man's mouth... she didn't want to believe it until the very very end.
But... this was the reality of it.
And the man standing before her was no longer her old crush... he was an enemy.
"I always thought you had a strong sense of justice and high ideals!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2394. You didn't become a politician for profit or glory!"

"In the old days, you saw your parents struggling to pay medical bills for chronic illness, so you wanted to become a politician and establish a medical insurance system for the needy!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2396. And yet, why are you resorting to this treachery?!"
Tomoe's sorrowful cries echoed through this otherwise quiet town.
The dreams that she heard from Tsukada when they were heading home from school together were especially pure and noble... She could feel the innocent youthful idealism behind them.
But despite that... no, because of that, she was fascinated with him.
While many people strive for things like that, most give up when they realize reaching it is impossible. But he continued to believe in it as he moved towards it...

At least among the people around Tomoe, nobody was as passionate about helping others as Tsukada. But because of that, Tomoe was disheartened to see him moving so far in the opposite direction. Angry, even.
"The drug causing these problems at the moment hasn't been licensed in Japan... Even worse, it's banned in the west!"
"Many patients took this medicine without knowing the risks, developed disorders as a result, and caused serious incidents!"
"Even now, people are still suffering side effects of that!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2400. And what's worse, that clinical data is being used to develop even more dangerous drugs?!"

"Your investigation has been thorough, as expected.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1077. The Alphabet Project... is part of a next-gen weapons development initiative researching the effects of chemical substances on the human spirit, currently being carried out by the Defense Agency and the Ministry of Health and Welfare."

"Sample microorganisms in Hinamizawa have been verified to possess just that.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1079. ...And clinical data on medicine that inhibits and enhances the human psyche is something pharmaceutical companies have been drooling for."
"So, by selling that to foreign companies at a high price, we can amass an enormous amount of wealth.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1081. ...That's the gold vein my boss has struck."
The Alphabet Project... and Hinamizawa?!
Hearing Tsukada spit that name out... and for the first time relating it to a place so deeply connected to Natsumi and Reina, had Tomoe's eyes open wide in shock.
And what was most shocking of all was... that a direct aide of Chiba would confirm the allegation of corruption without needing to be questioned...!
"Then... the company managing all this... is Lowell Inc?"
"...Lowell Inc is simply the recipient company in another country.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1083. The ones who requested the development data are from a much higher public institution."
"That's why even when the incident was discovered 13 years ago, and even when the Ministry of Health and Welfare bureaucrat was arrested 9 years ago, the leaked medical data was never uncovered."
"Chiba-sensei used Lowell Inc to bring newly developed high-risk drugs into the country, and cutting-edge drugs that hadn't been approved yet."
"Then had it prescribed it to patients without them knowing the risks... and sold the data from those clinical trials in order to reap a huge reward."
"That was the truth of the Lowell Incident back then... But, that's not all.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1088. Those backdoor deals are still continuing even today, just through a different route."
"I-it can't be...?!"
She had already heard the truth behind the Lowell Incident from Yamaoki. And that the incident ended without the true mastermind behind the conspiracy ever being caught...
But Tomoe couldn't imagine that the practice was still continuing even today...!
"Japan isn't some testing lab!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2406. And patients aren't guinea pigs!"
"To recognize that, mediate it, and turn a blind eye to it while lighting a fire that will cause more tragedy makes them merchants of death! The kind of people you should've hated most!"
"...And yet, why would someone like you ever get involved with them?!"
"To find the truth.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1090. And, to seek vengeance."
"V...vengeance?!"
"...It's not related to you."
After saying that, Tsukada pulled a cigarette from his inside pocket, lit it, and spit out a puff of smoke.
...Tobacco weakens your endurance, so he hated its very existence ever since his school days.
Nevertheless, the fact that he was so freely willing to smoke demonstrated just how much he'd changed, so Tomoe's chest painfully tightened.

"Hey, Minai.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1093. You should back away from this case as soon as you can.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1094. No matter how much you investigate this, it's not something you can stand up to on your own."
"No, this is even a foe the legal institutions of this country can't stand up to.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1096. ...So, defying them would be absolute madness."
"Is that your idea of justice?!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2411. That the strong should hurt and oppress the weak?!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2412. Didn't you hate that sort of world more than anyone?!"

"Then let me ask, what do you intend to do after this?@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1098. Report your findings back to the investigation headquarters you went through painstaking effort to establish? ...That much is fine."

"But the police have no authority to pursue and arrest government officials accused of corruption, especially right before an election.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1100. The police, even Public Security, can't stand up to a politician."
"Bureaucrats, police officers, the army...@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1102. History shows that there are no examples of those institutions standing on the side of the public."

"The law is structured to favor the powerful, and the more you cling to your earnest morals and ethics, the more you look a fool for it.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1104. ...Democracy is a convenient way for the strong to gain the obedience of the weak.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1105. And you guys are nothing but tools used in that endeavor."

"No... that's not true!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2415. We're public servants, protectors of the rights and the livelihood of all the people in our country!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2416. Not private soldiers following orders from politicians!"
"Don't gloss over it with lip service!@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1107. You don't know anything!"
That violent roar put her at a loss for words. Then, Tsukada gazed at Tomoe with a hateful look in his eyes, like he was about to pounce on her.

"Haven't you had enough of being beaten and pushed around, again and again?@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1109. Let me just say, Minai, I've seen far more of the scum of this world than you!@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1110. I was forced to witness it!"
"The people who callously abandon the lives of others for their own selfish reasons are cowards who will happily keep spinning together lies without feeling a shred of guilt!"
"And yet those scumbags will talk about the "motherland" and the "world" without any hesitation, just to justify and pad their own ego!"
"Hah, that's ridiculous! They're working for everyone's sake, they say!@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1114. But every last one of them is just looking out for number one!"
"Tsukada-san..."
"Even I... Even I always wanted to do something to make the world just a little better!"
"But the leaders of this country are cruel, slimy, tricksters!@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1117. Who think of nothing but their reputation and satisfying their own desires!"

"They'd even take down their own friends or family without any hesitation!@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1119. So, is it wrong for me to live like that?@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1120. Can you say you've been living a fair and proper enough life that you're justified in criticizing that?!"

"For you, it's easy...!@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1122. You can live for your own self-satisfaction, intoxicated by the name of justice!@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1123. Even if you fail, you can just say there was nothing more you could do, but there are always other cases so you'll do your best next time!"

"...But in our world, power itself is justice, and those who fall behind in the competition can never manage to crawl their way back up!@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1125. It takes power to actualize our ideals, and we can't afford to choose the means for that!!"
"...That's the conclusion you've reached?"
Contrary to Tsukada's fierce rage... Tomoe's heart was calm and cool.
...And sad.
...And lonely.
Back when he was in school, he had higher ideals than anyone and spared no effort in order to achieve them... but now, he was caught up in this theory of power, exposing his ugly, nasty form.
Did she really look up to a man like that?
Did a man like that really provide her moral support?
Even though he encouraged her and offered her advice so many times, she couldn't imagine hearing these words out of him...!
She wanted to understand the pain of knowing the difference between reality and ideals.
She wanted to understand how painful it is to know you're standing against unreasonable power and authority.
But... the person who taught her that was the man standing before her...
Her anger turned to disappointment... Nevertheless, the moment she stepped forward and faced Tsukada... she felt a strange feeling.
...That's wrong.
This man... was not the sort of coward who'd admit "defeat" in a situation like this. He was the kind of man who hated to show weakness in front of others.
But in that case... then why was he doing this?
"...So.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2426. That's how it is... That's what you think...?"
"What...?"
"You're... lying, aren't you?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2428. Tsukada-san.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2429. ...Deep in your heart, you don't really believe that, do you?"
After saying that.
Tomoe shook off the disgust and disappointment that had been nagging her just a moment ago, clinging... no, believing in the little hope she'd found as she softly gazed at Tsukada.
Maybe... she would be betrayed. Maybe she just got drunk on his past self in her memories, and was being completely off the mark.
However.
This was Tsukada... When Tomoe heard him say something he'd never say, a single possibility came to mind.
Or he might've had some other thought in mind when he approached her...
"What do you mean...?"

"It's strange.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2431. Abandoning your beliefs just to gain a title...? You care about your ideals and understand the hardships necessary to achieve them better than anyone else, so you'd never say something like that."
"Because your goal wasn't simply to become a politician, it was all the things you planned to do after becoming one... Isn't that what you told me?"
"Minai..."
"I'd like to believe... that I know what a selfless, fastidious person you are."

"So, if you really were disappointed with politics, you'd have chosen to take another path instead of becoming a politician.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2435. Politics can't be the only career path to achieving your ideals."
...She remembered.
Tsukada... had been a soldier of virtue, ever since he was in school.
A man who strongly believed in his ideals far more than the harshness of reality, and was willing to step forward to make them come true.
To imagine that he'd be blinded by self-interest and stray from that path... was unthinkable.
In fact, it would be more realistic to think he'd give up on politics and pursue medicine or religion.
"I did doubt you at first, it's true.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2437. And when you told me the facts, I was so surprised I almost lost hope in you."
"...But you approached me as a warning, didn't you?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2439. Because what other reason would you have to bother monologuing about your circumstances like we're in a TV drama?"
As she put the thoughts running through her head into words... Tomoe's hunch transformed into conviction.
Yes... she knew it. Tsukada was testing her.
He'd determine her real intentions, and decide how much to say based on that... even if it meant turning himself into the villain.
(...In the end, he hasn't changed at all.)
She chuckled at the strangeness of it. The way he sought out the thoughts and sense of values in others like this was exactly the same as old times...

"So... just what is it you're trying to do here?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2442. And why did you come talk to me?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2443. Were you prepared for me to be suspicious...? To be disappointed...?"

"Tsukada-san,@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2445. I may be an unreliable disciple,@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2446. but I'd like to think I've learned at least a little about living life with responsibility and determination."
"...So, please.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2448. Can you trust me?"
"Sheesh.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1132. ...Minai."
Tsukada suddenly let out a huge sigh, then shook his head and turned toward Tomoe.
...He had a calm, bitter smile on his face, as though he'd been freed of a possession. And the tension in the air dissipated, like a weight being lifted off their shoulders.
"You really haven't changed a bit.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1134. Even when I was in college... I never met anyone who trusted me as much as you."
"...Tsukada-san."

"I wonder why?@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1136. Ever since we were kids, there were so many people who said all sorts of bad things about me.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1137. But... for some reason, you were always on my side.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1138. And you always had a good grasp of what I was thinking behind the scenes.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1139. ...Why is that?"
changeReason: Old 'Even since old times' corrected to 'Ever since we were kids,' adding specificity about the childhood relationship implied by context.

TextENGNew: "Because I liked you... No, maybe more than that, because I respected you."
Tomoe looked away as she answered, smiling and reminiscing about the past.
Feeling isolated in any sort of group environment like school would make anyone anxious. That's why you learn to regulate yourself and make compromises to fit in, rather than rocking the boat.
...But a person like Tsukada who stood up for himself with strong beliefs and convictions would seem bizarre, and heretical... and in a sense, it would make him a target of envy.
Plus... Tsukada didn't expend additional effort to get along with people and behave in a friendly manner.
But on the other hand, he was very kind and sympathetic toward other people like himself who made an effort.
"Right after the incident with my parents... everyone was worried about me, yet treaded very carefully when they were around me."
"And once magazines began writing about the story, even people close to me started to keep their distance.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2453. ...But, you were different."
Even after she quit the team, even after she graduated... Tsukada continued worrying about how Tomoe was doing.

Maybe all she did with him was vent her complaints and share idle chitchat. Still, for someone like her who felt isolated from her classmates, it was the only place she could relieve the tension in her heart.
"Minai, after the incident you were always worried about your little sister, so you never showed any pain or sorrow on your face in public."

"And you stopped going to the club for her sake, and even changed career paths...@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1142. I really felt that was impressive.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1143. So, I thought it would be most polite to treat you on equal terms."

"You were always the sort of person who hated to compromise.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2455. You hated things being decided by committee and always stuck to your own beliefs... People often said you didn't cooperate at all."
"But you never wavered in your beliefs about the things you said.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2457. ...That's precisely the sort of person Japanese people hate."
"Hahaha, indeed... that may be true."
Tsukada let out a slightly lonely laugh while shaking his head and shrugging his shoulders.
...Opinions on strong, self-assertive people are often split down the middle in group activities.
Or rather, in this country's climate where harmony and cooperation are considered a virtue, overly aggressive personalities are frowned upon.
If anything, it might be similar to a form of envy for something they lack...
"...@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1146.If..."
Suddenly, Tsukada muttered something while sticking his cigarette into a portable ashtray. And then,

"If you'll permit me to say this.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1148. If I had stepped forward back then with the courage to ask you to go out with me.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1149. ...I wonder if things would have played out differently?@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1150. God can be cruel."
Tsukada pulled something out of his coat pocket and tossed it toward Tomoe with a snap of his wrist.
She grabbed it with both hands and held it under the streetlight as she opened them.
...It was apparently some sort of key, attached to an oval-shaped plate with numbers engraved into it.
"Tsukada-san, what is this...?"
"...It's insurance.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1152. If something happens to me in the future, please use it."
"Something happens...?"
"I'm sure you'll understand.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1154. Hey, Minai..."
"If it can be accomplished without bloodshed, that would be for the best.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1156. I still believe that, even now."
Unable to say anything, Tomoe just quietly stared at Tsukada.
What sort of meaning was hidden behind those words? ...But before she could think it over, he quickly smiled and turned around, then started walking.
"Anyway, I'll take you home.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1158. ...This is my car."
"Right. Huh...?!"
Just then.
When Tomoe saw Tsukada approach the driver's side door... she suddenly recalled the scene from half a year ago.
That's right. This parking lot... was very close to where Madoka's car exploded.
Then... No way?!
"H-hold on, Tsukada-san!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2465. The lock..."
The same moment that Tsukada turned back and answered... she could hear the kachink sound of the driver's side door unlocking.
But... it didn't explode.
"...What's wrong, Minai?"
"Huh?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2467. Oh, nothing... Sorry.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2468. Must've just been my imagination."
Tomoe smiled as she said that, then turned toward the passenger side.
And then, she cautiously placed her hand on the knob... and opened the door.
After confirming that nothing happened, Tomoe followed Tsukada's lead and sat down in the passenger seat. As she settled down into the seat... she felt a rush of relief.
"...Minai.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1162. You didn't think I was trying to kill you, did you?"
"Huh?!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2471. W-well, umm..."
"Well, I understand why you'd be suspicious.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1164. ...The truth is, I was ordered to come here and look into you."
"Really...?!"

"Akihiko Chiba actually considers you one of his greatest obstacles.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1166. ...In a sense, he's afraid.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1167. So, he told me to investigate your activities... and depending on the situation, to silence you."

"But unfortunately, I'm not doing it.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1169. Even if it meant you'd hate me for it, it would be best for you to lose interest in this investigation.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1170. ...And that thought was what drove me to threaten you like that.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1171. I'm sorry for saying such terrible things."
"It's all right... Enough about that.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2475. More importantly, I'm relieved to see you're still the same Tsukada-san as before."
"...@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1173.Hey, Minai.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1174. I've finally decided... I'm going to try believing in fate."
Tsukada put the key in the ignition... and paused for a while, carefully choosing his words, before turning toward Tomoe and giving her that answer.

"To be perfectly honest, I'm not sure how much I can actually do... I'm sorry that you had to get involved in this.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1176. But... I'd like to think getting to see you again was a good thing."
"Tsukada-san..."

"...But, Minai.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1178. I'm being complicit in wrongdoings, no doubt about that.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1179. Even I understand that this is acting beyond the scope of the law.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1180. So... when the time comes, you don't need to hesitate."
"...Right."
Understanding what he was implying... Tomoe quietly nodded while biting her lip.
If what Tsukada said earlier was true, there was no chance he'd be found innocent.
...But if that's the case, then the least level of kindness she could offer him is to handcuff him herself.
Feeling that resolve in her heart, Tomoe gently turned her gaze out the window.
...But then.
"...How nice.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1068. A revival of old love, huh?"
"Wha?!"
Even though nobody else should be in the car, the two of them turned toward the voice coming from the backseat. And then,
Something was inserted through the headrest and against the nape of Tsukada's neck, causing him to let out a moan as his body convulsed.
Then, his head slammed against the steering wheel with all of the momentum of his upper body behind it... and he stopped moving.
"Tsukada-san?!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2480. ...?!"
The moment she looked back, the enemy didn't waste that opportunity.
...She felt a numbness across her entire body, like she was struck by lightning... and then her consciousness quickly began fading away.
She saw the black mass emitting sparks and realized it was a stun gun... but it was the last thing she saw before her vision was swallowed by the darkness...
Tomoe opened her eyes, feeling a faint trembling in her neck.
...It was dark. No, not just that... the outlines of shapes were blurry and hard to make out.
She could tell she was inside a car based on the unique smell and feel of the chair... but everything outside the window looked like total darkness, so she had no idea where it was.
It felt like she was in a dream... like she'd been dragged to the depths of the darkness.
"Where... am... I...?"
"...Ah, so you're awake, Assistant Director.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1070. Or should I say... Minai's daughter."
She raised her head, which felt as heavy as lead, and turned toward the source of that voice.
The passenger side window was open about three centimeters... and in that gap... she felt she could see a familiar person's eyes.
"I should've expected you'd have a strong tolerance for alcohol.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1072. I never thought you'd wake up."
"H...Hanada-kun...?!"
Just then... a band of dazzling light gleamed into the car from the distance.
...And she faintly heard the high-pitched sound of a steam whistle.
That light just now must've been a lighthouse... so this is... a harbor?
And then...
"Hello, good evening.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1074. How does it feel waking up?"
The face reflected in the light as it shone past again was that of her subordinate, who supposedly went missing just a few days ago... Hanada.
Tomoe instinctively tried to lean forward... but was quickly struck by a feeling of dizziness and fell over in her seat.
There was no strength left in her body. A lethargic sensation enveloped her mind, and she could feel her vision and consciousness wavering.
If she relaxed her focus for even a moment, she'd close her eyes and fall asleep.

"Ah, you really should be careful.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1076. When you drink too much you end up in a drunken stupor.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1077. If you move around too much, it'll just make you feel more sick and drunk."
"D-drunk...?"
That's odd. She was feeling wary of Tsukada tonight, so she only had a small aperitif and didn't drink a drop of wine. And Tsukada drove, so he didn't have any alcohol at all...
That's right, where is Tsukada...?!
Slowly coming back to her senses, Tomoe turned toward the driver's seat beside her.
Tsukada was sitting there... with his eyes closed and not moving a muscle, as if he were dead.
"...?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2488. T-Tsuka...da-san...?!"

"It's all right, he's still alive.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1079. Apparently, mister secretary here wasn't as good at holding his liquor.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1080. ...Well, when you're injecting alcohol directly into someone's veins, it only takes a small amount to make you feel drunk right away."
Alcohol intoxication happens far sooner when injected into the bloodstream rather than being taken orally. Even a small amount will have an immediate effect on the whole body.

It was a fairly rudimentary disguise for making something look like a drunk driving accident... And depending on where you choose to inject it, it can be very difficult for forensics to notice the injection mark.

"You drank too much and then headed to the harbor hoping to sober up, but ended up steering the car into the sea.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1082. ...Even though you're a police officer. How terrible.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1083. You really have to be more careful.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1084. Heh heh heh...!"
"Y... you bastard...!!"
Tomoe squeezed out all her remaining strength to reach for the door handle and pull it. ...But despite her efforts, the door wouldn't budge.
"Oh, don't bother trying to open the door.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1086. I made some small modifications to the car...@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1087. and a few of the parts have been removed."
"Well, you might be able to escape if you manage to break the glass. But can you really pull that off when you're drunk...?"
In a fit of rage, Tomoe violently slammed on the door two or three times.
...But there was no chance something like that would break the lock. In fact, it only succeeded in making her dizziness worse... and an unpleasant sensation came rising up inside her chest.

"I told you, it's pointless.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1090. ...Anyway, the best thing you can do now is calm down and get some rest.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1091. You'll die more comfortably if you're not aware that you're drowning."
(G...give me a break!!)
Tomoe harshly spat that out while glaring at Hanada with a sharp gaze. And even though her movements were sluggish, she kept on slamming her body against the door.
"Good grief... you're quite the stubborn young lady."
Hanada shrugged his shoulders, then brought his face in closer to the window... and smiled as he kept talking.
"You went too far, Minai-san."

"If you had just focused your attention on the people who got sick and went crazy, you could've turned a blind eye to everything else.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1095. But once you learned about the existence of the medicine, there was no other choice."
"I was actually quite fond of you.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1097. ...But, well, orders are orders.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1098. No hard feelings, all right?"
"Orders...? From who?"
Hanada whistled like he was impressed by that question. Even moments before her death, this female detective was still thinking first and foremost about finding the culprit...?

"Honestly, I have no obligation to answer that.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1100. ...That's what I'd like to say, but let me offer you a parting gift.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1101. After all, it would be such a pain to come back as a ghost without ever knowing who it was that killed you.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1102. ...You see mister secretary over there? It was his master."
Tomoe caught her breath and froze, thinking the answer was more expected than surprising.
So... the mastermind behind this series of events... was Welfare Minister Akihiko Chiba?!
"N...no way...@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2497. So, twelve years ago... when my parents were murdered... f-for investigating the Lowell Incident... th-that was Chiba's doing, too...?!"
"...@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1104.Well, you might say so.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1105. But, the truth is... I'm the one who killed them."
A rush of numbness coursed through her body, like all the blood had drained out of her... and her vision suddenly darkened.
The flow of time had come to a halt, like she was stuck between two moments... She couldn't move a muscle. Her arms and legs were ensnared like the air itself was frozen.
On the other hand... her thoughts were finally starting to cool down, so she began organizing and piecing together this new information.
This man standing right before her eyes...
Was one of her subordinates... who always worked at her side, but...
He was the one... who burned down her parents' home... twelve years ago... An event that was so deeply engraved into her heart and mind...!!
"So?@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1107. How does it feel, standing face-to-face with the enemy you've been pursuing for so many years?@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1108. Heh heh heh...!!"
"Y-you bastaaaard...!!"
"Now, the time for idle chatter is over.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1110. ...Enjoy a nice short drive with your old sweetheart before you depart for the next world."
"It's a nonstop trip to the bottom of the sea.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1112. ...So, enjoy the ride."
Hanada moved toward the front of the car as he said that... and then crouched down, disappearing under the hood.
Just as the car began to lurch forward... she realized that he had removed the stoppers in front of the tires.
She stretched her leg across the driver's seat to step on the brake.
When she did, the car began slowly losing speed... so Tomoe tried to push even harder to bring it to a complete stop.
...But the alcohol coursing through Tomoe's head caused her consciousness to fade out for a moment, and as soon as the feeling in her foot disappeared... the car began moving once again.
Tomoe reinvigorated her fading consciousness and kicked her foot down hard to push on the brake once more.
...Watching this from the outside, it was probably quite humorous.
But if she relaxed for even a moment, her thoughts and consciousness would fade all at once, causing Tomoe and Tsukada to plummet into the sea locked inside this car...!

"...Hmph, an admirable effort.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1114. But the more you exert yourself, the more the alcohol will circulate through your body.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1115. It's pointless to struggle... but feel free to keep fighting to your heart's content.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1116. I'm terribly busy, so I'll be taking my leave now."
"H...Hanadaaaaaaaaa!!!"
"Farewell, milady."

"Your idealism was quite refreshing.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1119. Cheers to you for standing up to a cabinet minister with the weight of the world behind him.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1120. Ahahahahahahahahaha... aahahahahahahahahahahahah!!!"
With a boisterous laugh, Hanada slowly left the area. Tomoe gazed at his back as he did, with bloodlust in her eyes... then let out a sigh and turned toward Tsukada sitting beside her.
"Tsukada-senpai!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2505. Please wake up... Tsukada-san...!!"
Tomoe desperately held her foot down on the brake, while using all her remaining strength to shake Tsukada over and over again.
But... Tsukada didn't move.
His body temperature indicated he was still alive... but his arms, his legs, even his eyelids wouldn't budge.
"Please... wake up, Tsukada-san...!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2507. If... if we don't do something, then...!!"
No longer able to speak articulately, Tomoe barely squeezed that out in a heavy voice.
But... just as Hanada said, her movements had become sluggish, and to make matters worse her consciousness was dwindling... The darkness clouding her vision was getting deeper and deeper.
Her head was swirling and her thoughts scattered, like she was riding an amusement park ride.
And above all... their lives depended on the strength in her legs... but those legs didn't even feel like a part of her anymore.
She put a hand on the steering wheel in hopes of at least turning the car in another direction, but no matter how far she spun it the car kept moving straight.
Similarly, pulling up on the parking brake didn't bring the car to a stop... She didn't even feel any resistance behind it.
(Am I... going to die...?@k@-@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2510. Right here... in a place like this...?!)
She felt too sick... to even feel fear or sadness.
Her field of view blurred and distorted... She could no longer make out shapes, or even colors.
It felt like she was being dragged into the depths of the darkness.
Despite the extreme rage and agitation within her, she was rapidly losing energy and momentum.
...No, maybe...
The world she's experiencing right now is nothing but a dream... If she fell asleep right now, maybe everything would be back to normal after she woke up again.
...Yes.
This... has to be a dream.
So... even if I don't keep struggling in vain to stay awake... that's fine.
With those thoughts in mind... Tomoe's eyelids gradually began to feel heavier... and her field of view gradually became narrower.
I'm sorry, everyone.
Madoka.
Fujita-kun, Yamaoki-san, Maeno-san.
Natsumi-san, Rena-san.

The faces of people she knew came to mind, one after another... and she muttered an apology to each and every one of them... even as all the strength drained from her body... and eventually, even the feeling of holding down the brake started to feel weaker.
And then...
Then...
"...Minai-san!!"
Hearing that voice brought Tomoe back to her senses. She pried her eyelids open again and turned to face forward.
And then, right there...
It was impossible... but a girl who should no longer exist in this world... was standing right before her!
"Wh...why?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2513. Why are you...?"
"Minai-san, behind you!@k@vS14/35/VT4Bb_nagisa1073. The rear door!!@k@vS14/35/VT4Bb_nagisa1074. Break it!!!"
The girl had a look of desperation on her face, as she shouted and pointed behind Tomoe.
Before she could confirm the truth of this... her body began moving instinctively.
Tomoe kicked against the dashboard and used the momentum to slide her body into the gap between the two seats, making her way into the back seat.
The car began moving forward all the more quickly as soon as she released the brakes.
But she paid it no heed and used all the strength in her body to slam herself against the rear door...!
"Grrrr!!"
Again, and again!
"Uwaaaaahhh!!!"
When she slammed into the rear side door a second time... it let out a crunching sound as the door came open.
She courageously looked back at Tsukada with the feeling that she had done it... then turned back toward the wharf and took a deep breath.
"...May she rest in peace. It must be fate that she followed the same path as her parents."
"Haah, haah, haah...!!"
While holding Tsukada's body, Tomoe swam across the surface of the sea to a corner of the wharf... where thankfully, she spotted a ladder that led up to the surface. And then,
"Tsukada-san...!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2520. Hang in there, Tsukada-san...!!"
After shouting that... Tomoe's voice became very thin, and she coughed up all the water inside her throat.
Then, Tomoe collapsed... and completely lost consciousness.
